name: deck.gl (WebGL Geospatial Visualization)

auth.notes: Map tile providers may require API keys (Mapbox, Google Maps); deck.gl itself is free

performance.notes: WebGL2/WebGPU rendering; handles millions of data points at 60fps; GPU instancing

agent_readiness.notes: Uber's WebGL geospatial visualization framework. Layered architecture: base layers (ScatterplotLayer, PathLayer, PolygonLayer, ColumnLayer, ArcLayer, HexagonLayer, H3HexagonLayer), geo layers (GeoJsonLayer), and trips/animation layers. GPU instancing for millions of data points. Integrates with Mapbox GL, MapLibre, Google Maps. React and pure JS APIs. Kepler.gl is no-code tool built on deck.gl. pydeck provides Python Jupyter integration.

gotchas: WebGL expertise helps for custom layer development
Map provider API key needed for basemap tiles
Complex API for layering and interaction handling
best_when: Large-scale geospatial data visualization (millions of points)
avoid_when: Simple map markers (Leaflet or Mapbox GL sufficient)
summary: deck.gl is Uber's WebGL geospatial visualization framework for rendering millions of data points at 60fps. Layered architecture with ScatterplotLayer, HexagonLayer, H3HexagonLayer, GeoJsonLayer, ArcLayer, PathLayer. GPU instancing for performance. Integrates with Mapbox, MapLibre, Google Maps. React API. pydeck for Python/Jupyter. Kepler.gl is no-code deck.gl. Best for large-scale geospatial data like mobility, logistics, and location analytics.
